channel.title: flove making-of — quotas questions
channel.description: New quotas design questions from flove interviews.

item.title: What are the concrete per-tier hardware quotas for nety?
item.description:
• Light (mini/basic) — Minimal bandwidth/storage/compute — read-only or near-read-only. Low cost to run. • Medium (advanced/normal) — Moderate quotas — can contribute content, store some data, run light computations. • Full (super) — Unrestricted — full node, host others, maximum resources. • Defer again — Pin later after the lower tiers work. Keep it abstract for now.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Defer again
